text.div:
Generated Narrative: ImplementationGuide fhirems.emscore

FhirEMSCore

The official URL for this implementation guide is: 

https://fhirems.github.io/fhirEMSCoreIG/ImplementationGuide/fhirems.emscore

UNOFFICIAL AI-GENERATED RESEARCH PROJECT — NOT FOR CLINICAL USE. A FHIR R4 Implementation Guide for Emergency Medical Services (EMS) based on NEMSIS Version 3.5.1.251001CP2, aligned with US Core 6.1.0 and FHIR R4 base resources. This IG was generated by Claude (Anthropic) for public research and educational purposes. It is not endorsed by HL7, NEMSIS TAC, NHTSA, CMS, or any other standards body. The canonical URL is the project's GitHub Pages namespace and does not represent a registered HL7 namespace.

name: FhirEMSCore
title: FHIR EMS Core Implementation Guide (Unofficial Research Project)
description: UNOFFICIAL AI-GENERATED RESEARCH PROJECT — NOT FOR CLINICAL USE. A FHIR R4 Implementation Guide for Emergency Medical Services (EMS) based on NEMSIS Version 3.5.1.251001CP2, aligned with US Core 6.1.0 and FHIR R4 base resources. This IG was generated by Claude (Anthropic) for public research and educational purposes. It is not endorsed by HL7, NEMSIS TAC, NHTSA, CMS, or any other standards body. The canonical URL is the project's GitHub Pages namespace and does not represent a registered HL7 namespace.

grouping.name: Patient Care Report (PCR) Profiles
grouping.description: Core profiles representing an EMS Patient Care Report

grouping.name: EMS Response Resources
grouping.description: Profiles supporting EMS dispatch, response, and scene data

grouping.name: Clinical Assessment Profiles
grouping.description: Profiles for vitals, procedures, medications, and clinical findings

grouping.name: EMS Agency and Crew Profiles
grouping.description: Profiles for EMS agency demographics and crew members

grouping.name: Documentation and Custom Element Profiles
grouping.description: Profiles for PCR attachments, signatures, and agency custom elements (eOther, eCustom)

grouping.name: EMS Payment and Billing Profiles
grouping.description: Profiles for EMS billing, insurance, and CMS claims (ePayment section)

grouping.name: NEMSIS Extensions
grouping.description: Extensions required to represent NEMSIS-specific data elements

grouping.name: Value Sets
grouping.description: Value sets bound to NEMSIS coded elements

resource.name: Alternate Home Residence
resource.description: NEMSIS ePatient.22 - Indicates the patient's alternate home residence situation (e.g., homeless, migrant worker, foreign visitor).

resource.name: Barriers to Patient Care
resource.description: NEMSIS eHistory.01 - Barriers to the assessment or care of the patient (e.g., language, hearing impairment, unconscious). Required/National. Repeating (1..M). Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.

resource.name: EMS Abdomen Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.11 - Physical findings on abdominal assessment.

resource.name: EMS Abdominal Assessment Finding Location
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.10 - Location qualifier for abdominal assessment findings.

resource.name: EMS ACN Incident
resource.description: Automated Collision Notification (ACN) telematics data for NEMSIS eInjury.11–.29: providing company, incident identifiers, date/time, GPS location, vehicle description, impact and delta velocity data, rollover, seat location, seatbelt use, and airbag deployment reported by the vehicle telematics system.

resource.name: EMS ACN Vehicle Seat Location VS
resource.description: Automated Collision Notification (ACN) vehicle seat location of the patient (eInjury.26).

resource.name: EMS Additional Response Mode Descriptors
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.24 - Additional descriptors of the response mode (lights, sirens, speed, intersection navigation). Repeating.

resource.name: EMS Additional Response Mode Descriptors (ValueSet)
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.24 - Additional descriptors of response mode (lights, sirens, speed, intersection navigation).

resource.name: EMS Additional Transport Mode Descriptors
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.18 - Additional descriptors of transport mode (lights, sirens, speed, intersection navigation).

resource.name: EMS Advance Directives VS
resource.description: The type of advance directive documentation present for the patient (eHistory.05).

resource.name: EMS AED Use VS
resource.description: Whether an AED was used prior to EMS arrival (eArrest.07).

resource.name: EMS Age Units
resource.description: NEMSIS ePatient.16 - Units of measure for the patient's age when exact date of birth is unknown. Used alongside ePatient.15 (Age).

resource.name: EMS Agency Configuration
resource.description: NEMSIS dConfiguration: state, permitted procedures/medications with certification levels, protocols, specialty service capability, billing status, EMD provision/vendor, patient monitoring capability, crew call signs, and dispatch centers. Repeat the extension per configured state (dConfiguration.01).

resource.name: EMS Agency Details
resource.description:
Groups NEMSIS dAgency data elements that have no FHIR Organization base equivalent:
dAgency.09 (Primary Type of Service), dAgency.10 (Other Types of Service),
dAgency.11 (Level of Service), dAgency.12 (Organization Status).
All Mandatory or Required/National.

resource.name: EMS Agency Level of Service
resource.description: NEMSIS dAgency.11 - The highest level of care the EMS agency is licensed/certified to provide.

resource.name: EMS Agency Medical Director
resource.description: NEMSIS agency medical director credentials: degree (dContact.13), board certification type (dContact.14), compensation (dContact.15), and board certified/eligible status (dContact.16).

resource.name: EMS Agency Organization Status
resource.description: NEMSIS dAgency.12 - Whether the EMS agency is staffed by volunteers, paid personnel, or both.

resource.name: EMS Agency Organizational Type
resource.description: NEMSIS dAgency.13 - The organizational type of the EMS agency.

resource.name: EMS Agency Service Type
resource.description: NEMSIS dAgency.09/10 - The type(s) of EMS service provided by the agency.

resource.name: EMS AgencyContactType VS
resource.description: The role/type of the agency contact person (dContact.01).

resource.name: EMS AgencyLocationType VS
resource.description: The type of EMS agency location/station (dLocation.01).

resource.name: EMS Airbag Deployment VS
resource.description: Airbag deployment status observed at the crash (eInjury.08).

resource.name: EMS Airway Complications VS
resource.description: Complications encountered during airway management (eAirway.08).

resource.name: EMS Airway Confirmation Method VS
resource.description: Method used to confirm airway device placement (eAirway.04).

resource.name: EMS Airway Confirming Person VS
resource.description: Type of individual who confirmed the airway device placement (eAirway.06).

resource.name: EMS Airway Device VS
resource.description: Type of airway device being confirmed (eAirway.03).

resource.name: EMS Airway Failure Reasons VS
resource.description: Reasons for failed airway management (eAirway.09).

resource.name: EMS Airway Indications VS
resource.description: Indications for airway management intervention (eAirway.01).

resource.name: EMS Airway Management Context
resource.description: Contextual data for NEMSIS eAirway: indications, device confirmed, confirmation methods, confirming person, tube depth, crew member, complications, failure reasons, and decision/abandoned timestamps.

resource.name: EMS Airway Management Observation
resource.description: Documents an airway management event during an EMS response. NEMSIS eAirway covers advanced airway device placement, confirmation, complications, and failure reasons. eAirway.01–11 are captured primarily through ext-ems-airway-context. The observation code identifies the airway procedure type (SNOMED). The crew member (eAirway.07) is captured via performer.

resource.name: EMS Airway Placement Technique
resource.description: NEMSIS eProcedures.14 - Technique used for airway device placement.

resource.name: EMS Alcohol/Drug Use Indicators VS
resource.description: Indicators of potential patient alcohol or drug use (eHistory.17).

resource.name: EMS AllergyIntolerance
resource.description:
EMS allergy and intolerance record derived from US Core AllergyIntolerance.
Represents NEMSIS eHistory.06 (Medication Allergies, Recommended) and
eHistory.07 (Environmental/Food Allergies, Optional).
NEMSIS Version 3.5.1.251001CP2.

eHistory.06 (MedicationAllergies): substance from RxNorm.
eHistory.07 (Environmental/Food Allergies): substance from SNOMED CT
(NEMSIS stores SNOMED CT codes directly for eHistory.07).

NV applies to eHistory.06: NotRecorded → dataAbsentReason#not-asked;
NotApplicable → dataAbsentReason#not-applicable.
PN applies to eHistory.06: ExamFindingNotPresent, UnableToComplete
via ext-ems-pertinent-negative.

resource.name: EMS ALS Assessment Performed and Warranted
resource.description: NEMSIS ePayment.49. Whether an ALS assessment was performed and warranted.

resource.name: EMS Alternate Home Residence
resource.description: NEMSIS ePatient.22 - Codes describing a patient's alternate home residence situation when a standard home address is not applicable.

resource.name: EMS Ambulance Claim
resource.description: Represents a CMS ambulance claim generated from an EMS response. Derived from FHIR R4 Claim base resource (no US Core profile exists). Maps to NEMSIS ePayment billing elements: primary method of payment (ePayment.01), CMS service level (ePayment.50), response urgency (ePayment.40), transport assessment (ePayment.41), ambulance transport reason (ePayment.44), ambulance conditions indicators (ePayment.47), physician certification statement data (ePayment.02–07), mileage (ePayment.48), and supply items (ePayment.55/56).

resource.name: EMS Ambulance Conditions Indicator
resource.description: NEMSIS ePayment.47. CMS conditions indicators justifying ambulance necessity. Repeating.

resource.name: EMS Ambulance Conditions Indicator VS
resource.description: CMS ambulance conditions indicators justifying medical necessity (ePayment.47).

resource.name: EMS Ambulance Transport Reason
resource.description: NEMSIS ePayment.44. CMS ambulance transport reason code (A–E).

resource.name: EMS Ambulance Transport Reason VS
resource.description: CMS ambulance transport reason code (ePayment.44).

resource.name: EMS Arrest Etiology VS
resource.description: Etiology of the cardiac arrest event (eArrest.02).

resource.name: EMS Arrest First Responder VS
resource.description: Who first performed CPR, applied the AED, or defibrillated the patient (eArrest.20/21/22).

resource.name: EMS Arrest Witnessed VS
resource.description: Who witnessed the cardiac arrest (eArrest.04).

resource.name: EMS AVPU Level of Responsiveness
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.26 - The patient's level of responsiveness using the AVPU scale.

resource.name: EMS Back and Spine Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.14 - Physical findings on back and spine assessment.

resource.name: EMS Back and Spine Assessment Finding Location
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.13 - Location qualifier for back and spine assessment findings.

resource.name: EMS Barriers to Patient Care
resource.description: NEMSIS eHistory.01 - Barriers encountered during patient assessment or care.

resource.name: EMS Blood Pressure Measurement Method
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.08 - The technique used to obtain the blood pressure measurement. Recommended. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.

resource.name: EMS Blood Pressure Measurement Method (ValueSet)
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.08 - The technique used to measure blood pressure.

resource.name: EMS Blood Pressure Observation
resource.description:
EMS blood pressure panel derived from US Core Blood Pressure.
Represents NEMSIS eVitals.06 (SBP), eVitals.07 (DBP), and eVitals.08
(Method of Blood Pressure Measurement). Maps to LOINC 55284-4 (BP panel)
with SBP component (LOINC 8480-6) and DBP component (LOINC 8462-4).
NEMSIS Version 3.5.1.251001CP2.

resource.name: EMS Cardiac Arrest Condition
resource.description: Documents the presence (or absence) of cardiac arrest during an EMS response event. Follows NDR-009: the arrest status, etiology, resuscitation data, ROSC, and outcome are captured in the ext-ems-arrest-context extension. The FHIR Condition.code uses SNOMED CT 410429000 (Cardiac arrest).

resource.name: EMS Cardiac Arrest Context
resource.description: Contextual data for NEMSIS eArrest not directly representable in FHIR Condition: witnessed-by, resuscitation actions, AED use, CPR type, therapeutic hypothermia, ROSC, neurological outcome, end-of-event, and first-responder roles.

resource.name: EMS Cardiac Arrest VS
resource.description: Whether the patient was in cardiac arrest and when (eArrest.01).

resource.name: EMS Cardiac Rhythm
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.03 - Cardiac rhythm or ECG interpretation.

resource.name: EMS Caregiver Level
resource.description: NEMSIS eMedications.10 / eProcedures.10 - Level/role of the healthcare professional who administered the medication or performed the procedure.

resource.name: EMS Certification Level
resource.description: NEMSIS eCrew.02 - The certification/licensure level of the EMS crew member.

resource.name: EMS Certification Level (ValueSet)
resource.description: NEMSIS eCrew.02 - The certification/licensure level of the EMS crew member.

resource.name: EMS Chest Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.25 - Physical findings on chest assessment.

resource.name: EMS Chest Assessment Finding Location
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.24 - Location qualifier for chest assessment findings.

resource.name: EMS Chief Complaint Anatomic Location
resource.description: NEMSIS eSituation.07 - The anatomic location of the patient's chief complaint. Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.

resource.name: EMS Chief Complaint Anatomic Location (ValueSet)
resource.description: NEMSIS eSituation.07 - The anatomic location of the patient's chief complaint.

resource.name: EMS Chief Complaint Organ System
resource.description: NEMSIS eSituation.08 - The organ system involved in the patient's chief complaint.

resource.name: EMS Clinical Response to Treatment
resource.description: NEMSIS eMedications.07 / eProcedures.08 - Patient response to medication administration or procedure performance.

resource.name: EMS CMS Service Level
resource.description: NEMSIS ePayment.50. The CMS ambulance service level determining the base rate for billing.

resource.name: EMS CMS Service Level VS
resource.description: CMS ambulance service level for billing (ePayment.50).

resource.name: EMS Condition
resource.description:
Represents clinical conditions documented during an EMS encounter including
the provider's primary and secondary impressions (eSituation.11/12), primary
symptom (eSituation.09), and prior medical history (eHistory).
Derived from US Core Condition Encounter Diagnosis (Version 3.5.1.251001CP2).

For encounter diagnosis (primary/secondary impression): set category to
'encounter-diagnosis'. For prior medical history: set category to
'problem-list-item' or 'health-concern'.

resource.name: EMS Condition — Past Medical History
resource.description: Documents the patient's pre-existing medical/surgical history (eHistory.08) as reported during an EMS response. Condition.code carries the ICD-10-CM history code; advance directives, history source, immunizations, alcohol/drug indicators, pregnancy, and last oral intake are captured in the ext-ems-history-context extension. Create one resource per reported condition.

resource.name: EMS Core Server Capability Statement
resource.description: Defines the FHIR server capabilities required to implement the fhirEMSCore Implementation Guide. An EMS PCR repository SHALL support the profiles, interactions, and search parameters specified herein.

resource.name: EMS Coverage (Insurance)
resource.description: Represents insurance coverage information collected during an EMS response. Derived from FHIR R4 Coverage base resource (no US Core profile exists). Maps to NEMSIS ePayment insurance elements (ePayment.09–22, .57–60): insurance company identity, address, billing priority, policy and group IDs, insured person demographics, and payer type.

resource.name: EMS CPR Type VS
resource.description: Type of CPR care provided prior to EMS arrival (eArrest.09).

resource.name: EMS Crew Disposition
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.29 - What the crew did during the response.

resource.name: EMS Crew Member Response Role
resource.description: NEMSIS eCrew.03 - The role of the crew member during the EMS response.

resource.name: EMS Crew Safety
resource.description: Per-crew-member safety documentation from NEMSIS eOther: PPE used (eOther.03), crew member ID (eOther.04), suspected work-related exposure/injury/death (eOther.05), and its type (eOther.06). Repeat the extension once per crew member.

resource.name: EMS CrewPPE VS
resource.description: Personal protective equipment used by the EMS crew member (eOther.03).

resource.name: EMS Custom Result Target
resource.description: NEMSIS eCustomResults.03 - The CorrelationID of the PatientCareReport element or group that this custom element result applies to.

resource.name: EMS Destination Details
resource.description: Groups NEMSIS destination-selection and pre-arrival alert elements for the receiving facility. All clinical destination elements are Required/National.

resource.name: EMS Destination Location
resource.description:
Represents the facility or location to which the patient was transported.
Derived from US Core Location. Maps to NEMSIS eDisposition address elements
eDisposition.01–10 (Version 3.5.1.251001CP2). Destination type, capability,
and pre-arrival alert are captured in the EMSDestinationDetails extension on
the parent EMSEncounter.

resource.name: EMS Destination Team Pre-Arrival Alert
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.24 - Whether and what type of pre-arrival alert was given to the receiving facility.

resource.name: EMS DirectorCompensation VS
resource.description: Whether the medical director is compensated (dContact.15).

resource.name: EMS DisasterType VS
resource.description: Natural, suspected, intentional, or unintentional disaster associated with the event (eOther.07).

resource.name: EMS Discharge Disposition VS
resource.description: UB-04 patient discharge status codes used for ED disposition (eOutcome.01) and hospital disposition (eOutcome.02).

resource.name: EMS Dispatch Information
resource.description: Captures NEMSIS eDispatch supplementary fields: EMD performed (eDispatch.02, Required/National), EMD determinant code (eDispatch.03), dispatch center (eDispatch.04), dispatch priority (eDispatch.05), and CAD record ID (eDispatch.06).

resource.name: EMS Dispatch Priority (Patient Acuity)
resource.description: NEMSIS eDispatch.05 - The acuity level assigned at time of dispatch.

resource.name: EMS Dispatch Reason
resource.description: NEMSIS eDispatch.01 - The dispatch complaint or reason for response.

resource.name: EMS Dispatch Reason (ValueSet)
resource.description: NEMSIS eDispatch.01 - The dispatch complaint or reason for the EMS response. Extensible to allow agency-specific or SNOMED CT dispatch codes.

resource.name: EMS Disposition Codes
resource.description:
Groups the core NEMSIS disposition data elements. All sub-elements are
Mandatory or Required/National. Nillable sub-elements accept NV codes via
ext-ems-not-value when the value is not recorded or not applicable.

resource.name: EMS Disposition Instructions Provided
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.26 - Instructions provided to the patient at disposition.

resource.name: EMS Document Reference
resource.description: An external electronic document or file attachment associated with the EMS Patient Care Report (eOther.09–.11, .22): diagnostic images, ECG/lab results, DNR forms, refusal sheets, and similar. The NEMSIS document type (4509xxx) is carried in ext-ems-document-type; DocumentReference.type keeps the US Core LOINC binding.

resource.name: EMS ECG Context
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.04 (ECG Type) and eVitals.05 (Method of ECG Interpretation). Both Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.

resource.name: EMS ECG Interpretation Method
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.05 - How the ECG was interpreted.

resource.name: EMS ECG Type
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.04 - The number of leads used for ECG acquisition.

resource.name: EMS EMD Performed
resource.description: NEMSIS eDispatch.02 - Whether Emergency Medical Dispatch pre-arrival instructions were provided.

resource.name: EMS EMDProvided VS
resource.description: Whether EMD is provided to the agency service area (dConfiguration.13).

resource.name: EMS Encounter
resource.description:
EMS-specific constraints on US Core Encounter representing a single EMS response/call.
Covers NEMSIS eResponse (unit/agency, response mode, delays), eTimes (all 19 timestamps),
eDispatch (dispatch complaint, EMD, priority), and eDisposition (unit, crew, transport,
destination, level of care) sections. NEMSIS Version 3.5.1.251001CP2.

resource.name: EMS End of Cardiac Arrest Event VS
resource.description: Disposition of the patient at the end of the EMS cardiac arrest event (eArrest.18).

resource.name: EMS External Document Type
resource.description: NEMSIS eOther.09 - The type of external electronic document, using NEMSIS 4509xxx codes. Complements DocumentReference.type (US Core-required LOINC binding).

resource.name: EMS External Report Type VS
resource.description: Type of external report associated with the EMS event (eOutcome.03).

resource.name: EMS ExternalDocumentType VS
resource.description: Type of external electronic document associated with the PCR (eOther.09).

resource.name: EMS Extremities Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.16 - Physical findings on extremities assessment.

resource.name: EMS Extremity Assessment Finding Location
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.15 - Location qualifier for extremity assessment findings.

resource.name: EMS Eye Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.18 - Physical findings on eye assessment, including pupil size and reactivity.

resource.name: EMS Eye Assessment Finding Location
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.17 - Laterality qualifier for eye assessment findings.

resource.name: EMS Face Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.06 - Physical findings on face assessment.

resource.name: EMS First Monitored Rhythm VS
resource.description: First monitored cardiac rhythm by this EMS unit (eArrest.11). Subset for initial arrest rhythms. For full rhythm list including post-ROSC, see EMSCardiacRhythmVS.

resource.name: EMS GCS Qualifier
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.22 - Qualifiers documenting conditions that affect GCS scoring validity.

resource.name: EMS Glasgow Coma Score (GCS) Panel
resource.description:
GCS panel Observation derived from US Core Observation Clinical Result.
Represents NEMSIS eVitals.19 (Eye), eVitals.20 (Verbal), eVitals.21 (Motor),
eVitals.22 (Qualifier), and eVitals.23 (Total GCS).

Pattern (NDR per CLAUDE.md): parent GCS panel (LOINC 9269-2) with three
hasMember references to individual Eye/Verbal/Motor sub-Observations, plus
a qualifier component. Sub-observations use this same profile.

LOINC codes: Total=9269-2, Eye=9267-6, Verbal=9268-4, Motor=9266-8.
GCS scores are integers: Eye 1–4, Verbal 1–5, Motor 1–6.
NEMSIS Version 3.5.1.251001CP2.

resource.name: EMS Head Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.05 - Physical findings on head assessment.

resource.name: EMS Heart Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.09 - Physical findings on heart/cardiac auscultation.

resource.name: EMS History Context
resource.description: Contextual data for NEMSIS eHistory not directly representable in FHIR Condition: advance directives, patient's practitioner name, history source, immunization type/year, emergency information form, alcohol/drug use indicators, pregnancy status, and last oral intake.

resource.name: EMS Home Medication Dosage Units VS
resource.description: Units for the patient's current (home) medication dose (eHistory.14). Distinct from the eMedications.06 administered-dose units (3706xxx codes).

resource.name: EMS Home Medication Frequency VS
resource.description: Dosing frequency for the patient's current (home) medications (eHistory.20).

resource.name: EMS Hospital Capability
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.23 - Specialty capabilities of the receiving facility.

resource.name: EMS Hospital In-Patient Destination
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.22 - The specific in-hospital unit or bed type the patient was delivered to.

resource.name: EMS Imaging Study Type VS
resource.description: Type of imaging study obtained (eLabs.05).

resource.name: EMS Immunization Type VS
resource.description: The patient's immunization history type (eHistory.10).

resource.name: EMS Initial Patient Acuity
resource.description: NEMSIS eSituation.13 - Patient acuity at initial EMS contact.

resource.name: EMS Injury Condition
resource.description: Documents an injury and its circumstances during an EMS response event. Condition.code carries the cause of injury (eInjury.01, ICD-10-CM external cause codes). Mechanism of injury, CDC trauma triage criteria, vehicle collision details, safety equipment, and fall height are captured in the ext-ems-injury-context extension; Automated Collision Notification (ACN) telematics data in ext-ems-acn-incident.

resource.name: EMS Injury Context
resource.description: Contextual data for NEMSIS eInjury not directly representable in FHIR Condition: mechanism of injury, CDC trauma triage criteria (high and moderate risk), vehicle impact area, patient location in vehicle, occupant safety equipment, airbag deployment, height of fall, and OSHA personal protective equipment.

resource.name: EMS Insurance Billing Priority VS
resource.description: Insurance company billing priority (ePayment.11).

resource.name: EMS Insured Relationship VS
resource.description: Patient's relationship to the insured (ePayment.22).

resource.name: EMS Lab and Imaging Observation
resource.description: Documents lab results and imaging study results obtained during an EMS response. Covers NEMSIS eLabs.01–08. All eLabs elements are Optional. Lab results (eLabs.03/04) are represented as individual Observations; imaging study results (eLabs.05/06) are represented similarly with appropriate LOINC codes.

resource.name: EMS Lab Result Type VS
resource.description: Type of lab result obtained (eLabs.03).

resource.name: EMS Length Based Tape Measure (Broselow-Luten)
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.02 - Broselow-Luten color zone for length-based weight estimation.

resource.name: EMS Level of Care Provided per Protocol
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.32 - The level of care (BLS, ALS, Critical Care, etc.) authorized by protocol.

resource.name: EMS Location — Agency Station
resource.description: An EMS agency location — headquarters, station, or staging area (NEMSIS dLocation). One resource per agency location, managed by the EMSOrganization.

resource.name: EMS Lung Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.23 - Auscultatory and respiratory findings on lung assessment.

resource.name: EMS Lung Assessment Finding Location
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.22 - Laterality qualifier for lung assessment findings.

resource.name: EMS MCI Triage Classification
resource.description: NEMSIS eScene.08 - START/JumpSTART triage classification for MCI patient.

resource.name: EMS Mechanism of Injury VS
resource.description: The mechanism of the event which caused the injury (eInjury.02).

resource.name: EMS Medical History Obtained From VS
resource.description: The source of the patient's medical history information (eHistory.09).

resource.name: EMS MedicalDirectorBoardCert VS
resource.description: The agency medical director board certification type (dContact.14).

resource.name: EMS MedicalDirectorDegree VS
resource.description: The agency medical director degree (dContact.13).

resource.name: EMS Medication Administered Route
resource.description: NEMSIS eMedications.04 - The route by which the medication was administered.

resource.name: EMS Medication Administration
resource.description:
EMS medication administration derived directly from FHIR R4
MedicationAdministration. No US Core profile exists for this resource
(NDR-008). Represents the NEMSIS eMedications section
(Version 3.5.1.251001CP2).

NEMSIS eMedications records what was ACTUALLY administered in the field,
not what was ordered. Always use MedicationAdministration, never
MedicationRequest, for NEMSIS eMedications data.

eMedications.03 uses RxNorm codes (direct pass-through from NEMSIS).
Additional NEMSIS context (prior EMS care, response, complication,
authorization, by-another-unit) is carried in EMSMedicationContext.

resource.name: EMS Medication Administration Context
resource.description: Contextual data for NEMSIS eMedications not directly representable in FHIR MedicationAdministration: prior EMS care indicator, clinical response, complication(s), and authorization type.

resource.name: EMS Medication Complication
resource.description: NEMSIS eMedications.08 - Adverse reactions or complications occurring with medication administration.

resource.name: EMS Medication Dosage Units
resource.description: NEMSIS eMedications.06 - Units of measurement for medication dosage.

resource.name: EMS Medication Statement — Home Medications
resource.description: Documents a medication the patient reports currently taking (eHistory.12 Current Medications), with dose (eHistory.13/.14), route (eHistory.15), and frequency (eHistory.20). Distinct from EMSMedicationAdministration, which records medications administered during the EMS encounter. One resource per reported medication.

resource.name: EMS Mental Status Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.19 - Mental status and cognitive assessment findings.

resource.name: EMS Mileage to Closest Hospital
resource.description: NEMSIS ePayment.48. Distance in miles to the closest appropriate hospital facility.

resource.name: EMS MonitoringCapability VS
resource.description: Patient monitoring capabilities of the agency (dConfiguration.15).

resource.name: EMS Neck Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.07 - Physical findings on neck assessment.

resource.name: EMS Neurological Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.20 - Neurological assessment findings including strength, speech, coordination, and seizures.

resource.name: EMS Neurological Outcome VS
resource.description: Cerebral performance category (CPC) at hospital discharge (eArrest.13).

resource.name: EMS Occupant Safety Equipment VS
resource.description: Occupant safety equipment in use by the patient at the time of the injury (eInjury.07).

resource.name: EMS Organization
resource.description:
EMS agency organizational information derived from US Core Organization.
Maps to NEMSIS dAgency section (Version 3.5.1.251001CP2). Captures agency
identifiers, name, state, organizational type, service type, and level of service.

resource.name: EMS OSHA Personal Protective Equipment VS
resource.description: OSHA-defined personal protective equipment in use by the patient at the time of a work-related injury (eInjury.10).

resource.name: EMS Other Context
resource.description: Encounter-level NEMSIS eOther data: review requested, potential system-of-care/registry candidacy, disaster type, and the crew member completing the report.

resource.name: EMS Outcome Observation
resource.description: Documents hospital and ED outcome data collected by the EMS agency. Covers NEMSIS eOutcome.01 (ED Disposition), eOutcome.02 (Hospital Disposition), eOutcome.09/12 (ED/Hospital Procedures using ICD-10-PCS), eOutcome.10/13 (ED/Hospital Diagnoses using ICD-10-CM), eOutcome.11 (Hospital Admission DateTime), eOutcome.16 (Hospital Discharge DateTime), eOutcome.18 (ED Admission DateTime), and eOutcome.21 (Injury Severity Score). Each significant eOutcome data point is captured as an Observation component.

resource.name: EMS Pain Scale Type
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.28 - The pain assessment tool used. Recommended. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.

resource.name: EMS Pain Scale Type (ValueSet)
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.28 - The type of pain scale used to assess the patient's pain.

resource.name: EMS Patient
resource.description:
Constraints on US Core Patient for the NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2 ePatient section
(25 elements). Adds three EMS-specific extensions: census tract (ePatient.11),
alternate home residence (ePatient.22), and estimated age (ePatient.15/16) for
use when date of birth is unknown. See the EMS Patient mapping page for the
full NEMSIS element → FHIR cross-reference.

resource.name: EMS Patient Acuity Upon Release
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.19 - The patient's acuity triage level at the time of EMS release.

resource.name: EMS Patient Care Report (PCR)
resource.description:
Represents an EMS Patient Care Report (PCR) as a FHIR Composition.
This is the root document that ties together all sections of an EMS
call. Based on NEMSIS eRecord and overall PCR structure
(Version 3.5.1.251001CP2).

Section codes: Standard LOINC codes are used where a clear analog
exists. NEMSIS-specific section codes (nemsis-codes#section-*) are
used for EMS-only sections without a well-matched LOINC code.

Mandatory NEMSIS sections (1..1): eResponse, eTimes, ePatient,
eScene, eSituation, eDisposition.
Optional NEMSIS sections (0..1 or 0..*): eHistory, eVitals,
eProcedures, eMedications, eExam, eAirway, eArrest, eNarrative.

resource.name: EMS Patient Estimated Age
resource.description:
Captures NEMSIS ePatient.15 (Age value) and ePatient.16 (Age Units) when the
patient's date of birth (ePatient.17) is unknown. Both source elements are
Required/National. When birthDate is known, derive age computationally at the
time of the encounter; this extension is not needed in that case.

resource.name: EMS Patient Evaluation/Care
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.28 - What evaluation and care was provided to the patient.

resource.name: EMS Patient Transport Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS ePayment.41. Physical assessment of the patient justifying ambulance transport. Repeating.

resource.name: EMS Patient Transport Assessment VS
resource.description: Patient's physical condition that necessitated ambulance transport (ePayment.41).

resource.name: EMS PatientRepresentative VS
resource.description: The relationship of the patient representative who signed (eOther.14).

resource.name: EMS Payer Type
resource.description: NEMSIS ePayment.57. X12 payer type code for the insurance company.

resource.name: EMS Payer Type VS
resource.description: X12 payer type codes for the insurance company (ePayment.57).

resource.name: EMS Pelvis/Genitourinary Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.12 - Physical findings on pelvic/genitourinary assessment.

resource.name: EMS Physical Exam Observation
resource.description:
Physical examination findings Observation derived from US Core
Observation Clinical Result. Represents NEMSIS eExam section
assessments (Version 3.5.1.251001CP2).

One Observation per body region assessment. Use Observation.code
to identify the body region (LOINC or SNOMED CT when available;
NEMSIS exam category code otherwise). Use Observation.bodySite
for location qualifiers (eExam.10, .13, .15, .17, .22, .24).
Each discrete finding is a component with valueCodeableConcept.
Multiple findings for the same region → multiple components.

Applicable NEMSIS elements:
  eExam.01: Estimated Body Weight → valueQuantity (kg)
  eExam.02: Length Tape Measure → valueCodeableConcept from EMSExamLengthTapeMeasureVS
  eExam.03: Date/Time of Assessment → effectiveDateTime
  eExam.04–25: Body region assessments → component.valueCodeableConcept

All eExam elements are Optional in NEMSIS.
NV: eExam.01, eExam.02, eExam.21 support NotRecorded / NotApplicable.
PN: Most body region assessment elements (eExam.04–25) support ExamFindingNotPresent.

resource.name: EMS Practitioner
resource.description:
EMS personnel demographics and licensure information derived from US Core Practitioner.
Maps to NEMSIS dPersonnel section (Version 3.5.1.251001CP2). Captures name, state
licensure, certification level, and demographic information.

resource.name: EMS PractitionerRole
resource.description:
Represents an EMS crew member's role during a specific EMS response.
Derived from US Core PractitionerRole. Maps to NEMSIS eCrew section
(eCrew.01–03) combined with dPersonnel certification data (Version 3.5.1.251001CP2).
One PractitionerRole resource per crew member per response.

resource.name: EMS Pregnancy Status VS
resource.description: The patient's pregnancy status (eHistory.18).

resource.name: EMS Primary Payment Method
resource.description: NEMSIS ePayment.01. Primary method of payment for the EMS response.

resource.name: EMS Primary Payment Method VS
resource.description: Primary method of payment for the EMS response (ePayment.01).

resource.name: EMS Procedure
resource.description:
EMS procedure derived from US Core Procedure. Represents the NEMSIS
eProcedures section (Version 3.5.1.251001CP2). Each procedure in a
NEMSIS ProcedureGroup maps to one FHIR Procedure resource.

eProcedures.03 uses SNOMED CT codes (direct pass-through from NEMSIS).
Complications (eProcedures.07) map to Procedure.complication.
Outcome/success (eProcedures.06) maps to Procedure.outcome.
Vascular access site (eProcedures.13) maps to Procedure.bodySite.
Additional NEMSIS context (prior EMS care, attempt count, response,
authorization, airway technique, by-another-unit) is carried in
the EMSProcedureContext extension.

resource.name: EMS Procedure Complication
resource.description: NEMSIS eProcedures.07 - Adverse events or complications occurring with procedure performance.

resource.name: EMS Procedure Context
resource.description: Contextual data for NEMSIS eProcedures not directly representable in FHIR Procedure: prior EMS care indicator, number of attempts, clinical response, authorization type, airway technique(s), and performed-by-another-unit indicator.

resource.name: EMS Protocol Used
resource.description: NEMSIS eProtocols.01 - Protocol used during the EMS response.

resource.name: EMS Protocols Used
resource.description: NEMSIS eProtocols.01 - EMS protocol(s) followed during the response.

resource.name: EMS Provenance — PCR Signature
resource.description: Records a signature event on the EMS Patient Care Report (eOther.12–.21): who signed, in what role, for what reason, with what outcome. The FHIR Signature datatype carries the signature graphic (eOther.18 → signature.data) and time (eOther.19 → signature.when); NEMSIS signer/reason/status metadata is carried in ext-ems-signature-context. One Provenance per signature event.

resource.name: EMS Pulse Rhythm
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.13 - The regularity of the patient's pulse.

resource.name: EMS Questionnaire Response — Custom Element Results
resource.description: Carries collected values for agency- or state-specific custom data elements (NEMSIS eCustomResults) per NDR-010, answering an EMSQuestionnaire definition.

resource.name: EMS Questionnaire — Custom Element Definitions
resource.description: Defines agency- or state-specific custom data elements (NEMSIS eCustomConfiguration) per NDR-010. Each eCustomConfiguration entry becomes one Questionnaire.item; collected values are reported via EMSQuestionnaireResponse.

resource.name: EMS Reason CPR Discontinued VS
resource.description: Reason CPR or resuscitation was discontinued (eArrest.16).

resource.name: EMS Reason for Choosing Destination
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.20 - The reason the destination was chosen.

resource.name: EMS Reason for Interfacility Transfer
resource.description: NEMSIS eSituation.20 - The clinical reason for an interfacility transfer or transport.

resource.name: EMS Reason for Refusal/Release
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.31 - Reason documented when patient refused care or was released.

resource.name: EMS RegistryCandidate VS
resource.description: Potential system of care, specialty, or registry candidacy for the patient (eOther.02).

resource.name: EMS Reperfusion Checklist
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.31 - Contraindication status for thrombolytic therapy per the reperfusion checklist.

resource.name: EMS Respiratory Effort
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.15 - The quality of the patient's respiratory effort.

resource.name: EMS Response Delays
resource.description:
Groups the five NEMSIS delay type elements (eResponse.08–12), each
Required/National with repeating values and NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.

resource.name: EMS Response Mode to Scene
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.23 - The response mode (emergent vs non-emergent) used by the EMS unit en route to the scene.

resource.name: EMS Response Mode to Scene (ValueSet)
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.23 - The response mode (emergent vs non-emergent) used en route to the scene.

resource.name: EMS Response Times
resource.description: Captures the full set of NEMSIS eTimes timestamps for a single EMS response.

resource.name: EMS Response Type
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.05 - The type of EMS response (Emergency/Non-Emergency).

resource.name: EMS Response Type (ValueSet)
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.05 - The type of EMS response.

resource.name: EMS Response Urgency
resource.description: NEMSIS ePayment.40. Whether the EMS response was immediate or non-immediate.

resource.name: EMS Response Urgency VS
resource.description: Urgency of the EMS response for billing purposes (ePayment.40).

resource.name: EMS Resuscitation Attempted VS
resource.description: Type of resuscitation attempted by the EMS unit (eArrest.03).

resource.name: EMS ROSC VS
resource.description: Whether and when return of spontaneous circulation occurred (eArrest.12).

resource.name: EMS Scene Details
resource.description:
Groups key NEMSIS eScene data elements that have no FHIR Location base equivalent:
eScene.01 (First EMS Unit on Scene), eScene.06 (Number of Patients at Scene),
eScene.07 (Mass Casualty Incident), eScene.08 (MCI Triage Classification),
eScene.13 (Incident Facility or Location Name). All Required/National unless noted.

resource.name: EMS Scene GPS Location
resource.description: NEMSIS eScene.11 - GPS coordinates of the EMS scene.

resource.name: EMS Scene Location
resource.description:
Represents the physical location where an EMS incident occurred.
Derived from US Core Location. Maps to NEMSIS eScene section
(Version 3.5.1.251001CP2). Captures scene address, GPS coordinates,
incident location type, and scene context (number of patients, MCI status).

resource.name: EMS Seat Row Location VS
resource.description: The seat row location of the patient in the vehicle at the time of the crash (eInjury.06).

resource.name: EMS Sex
resource.description:
NEMSIS ePatient.25 - The patient's sex as recorded by EMS.
Used for documentation and mapping to Patient.gender and the us-core-birthsex extension.
Note: ePatient.13 (Gender) is DEPRECATED in NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2 — use ePatient.25 (Sex) instead.

resource.name: EMS Signature Context
resource.description: NEMSIS signature metadata for the PCR: type of person signing (eOther.12), signature reason (eOther.13), patient representative type (eOther.14), signature status (eOther.15), signature file name/type (eOther.16/.17), and signer name (eOther.20/.21).

resource.name: EMS SignatureReason VS
resource.description: The reason a signature was obtained (eOther.13).

resource.name: EMS SignatureStatus VS
resource.description: The status/disposition of the signature (eOther.15).

resource.name: EMS SignerType VS
resource.description: The type of person signing (eOther.12).

resource.name: EMS Skin Assessment
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.04 - Physical findings on skin assessment.

resource.name: EMS SpecialtyServiceCapability VS
resource.description: EMS agency specialty service capabilities (dConfiguration.11).

resource.name: EMS Stroke Scale Context
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.30 (Stroke Scale Type, Required/National) and eVitals.34 (Stroke Scale Score, Recommended). Used alongside the stroke scale result Observation.

resource.name: EMS Stroke Scale Result
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.29 - The result of the prehospital stroke scale assessment.

resource.name: EMS Stroke Scale Type
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.30 - The prehospital stroke screening tool used.

resource.name: EMS Stroke/CVA Symptoms Resolved
resource.description: NEMSIS eExam.21 - Whether stroke/CVA symptoms resolved prior to or during EMS care.

resource.name: EMS Transport Details
resource.description: NEMSIS transport method and mode elements: eDisposition.16 (EMS Transport Method), eDisposition.17 (Transport Mode from Scene), and eDisposition.18 (Additional Transport Mode Descriptors). All Required/National.

resource.name: EMS Transport Disposition
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.30 - Whether/how the patient was transported.

resource.name: EMS Transport Disposition (Legacy)
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.30 - Whether/how the patient was transported. Superseded by EMSDispositionCodes complex extension in EMSEncounter.

resource.name: EMS Transport Method
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.16 - The vehicle/mode used to transport the patient.

resource.name: EMS Transport Mode from Scene
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.17 - Emergent vs non-emergent transport mode from scene.

resource.name: EMS Trauma Triage High Risk VS
resource.description: Trauma triage criteria indicating high risk for serious injury — CDC Field Triage Guideline red criteria (injury patterns and physiologic instability) (eInjury.03).

resource.name: EMS Trauma Triage Moderate Risk VS
resource.description: Trauma triage criteria indicating moderate risk for serious injury — CDC Field Triage Guideline yellow criteria (mechanism of injury and EMS judgment) (eInjury.04).

resource.name: EMS Treatment Authorization Type
resource.description: NEMSIS eMedications.11 / eProcedures.11 - How the medication administration or procedure was authorized.

resource.name: EMS Type of Destination
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.21 - The type of destination the patient was transported to.

resource.name: EMS Type of Dispatch Delay
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.08 - Types of dispatch delay.

resource.name: EMS Type of Response Delay
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.09 - Types of response delay en route to scene.

resource.name: EMS Type of Scene Delay
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.10 - Types of delay at the scene.

resource.name: EMS Type of Transport Delay
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.11 - Types of delay during transport.

resource.name: EMS Type of Turn-Around Delay
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.12 - Types of delay returning to service after transport.

resource.name: EMS Unit Disposition
resource.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.27 - Whether/how the unit made patient contact.

resource.name: EMS Unit Type
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.07 - The type of EMS vehicle/unit.

resource.name: EMS Unit Type (ValueSet)
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.07 - The type of EMS vehicle/unit.

resource.name: EMS Vascular Access Location
resource.description: NEMSIS eProcedures.13 - Anatomical site of vascular access (IV/IO line).

resource.name: EMS Vehicle Device
resource.description:
EMS vehicle/unit Device derived directly from FHIR R4 Device.
No US Core profile exists for Device. Represents NEMSIS dVehicle
section (Version 3.5.1.251001CP2) — agency-level demographics
about the EMS vehicle/unit.

dVehicle elements that are Optional or Recommended are represented
as MS only when Recommended per NEMSIS.

resource.name: EMS Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) Identifier System
resource.description: Identifier system for the 17-character Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) used to uniquely identify EMS vehicles. NEMSIS dVehicle.02.

resource.name: EMS Vehicle Type
resource.description: NEMSIS dVehicle.04 - The type of EMS vehicle.

resource.name: EMS Vehicle Unit Identification
resource.description: NEMSIS eResponse.13 (EMS Vehicle/Unit Number) and eResponse.14 (EMS Unit Call Sign). Both Mandatory/National.

resource.name: EMS Vital Obtained Prior to EMS Care
resource.description: NEMSIS eVitals.02 - Whether this vital sign set was obtained by another provider prior to this EMS unit's care. Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.

resource.name: EMS Vital Signs Observation
resource.description:
Parent profile for all EMS vital sign observations. Derived from US Core Vital Signs.
Covers NEMSIS eVitals section (Version 3.5.1.251001CP2). Each vital sign set in
NEMSIS (VitalGroup) produces one Observation per element per group (NDR-007).

Use this profile directly for: Heart Rate (eVitals.10), Respiratory Rate (eVitals.14),
Pulse Oximetry (eVitals.12), Body Temperature (eVitals.24), Blood Glucose (eVitals.18),
ETCO2 (eVitals.16), Carbon Monoxide (eVitals.17), AVPU (eVitals.26), Pain Score
(eVitals.27), Cardiac Rhythm (eVitals.03), Stroke Scale (eVitals.29), Reperfusion
Checklist (eVitals.31), Pulse Rhythm (eVitals.13), Respiratory Effort (eVitals.15).

Use EMSObservationBP for Blood Pressure (eVitals.06/07 — component panel).
Use EMSObservationGCS for Glasgow Coma Score (eVitals.19–23 — hasMember panel).

LOINC codes for each vital element (from CLAUDE.md terminology strategy):
  HR:          8867-4     SpO2:       59408-5    Resp Rate:   9279-1
  SBP (comp):  8480-6     DBP (comp): 8462-4     Temp:        8310-5
  ETCO2:       19889-5    BG:         59469-7    Weight:      29463-7
  AVPU:        67775-7    Pain:       72514-3    GCS Total:   9269-2
  GCS Eye:     9267-6     GCS Verbal: 9268-4     GCS Motor:   9266-8

resource.name: EMS WorkInjuryExposure VS
resource.description: Type of crew work-related injury, death, or suspected exposure (eOther.06).

resource.description: Example agency station demonstrating the EMSLocationAgency profile: Riverside County Fire Station 7 in Murrieta, CA, with GPS, USNG, and station number.

resource.description: Example airway management observation demonstrating the EMSObservationAirway profile. Endotracheal intubation performed on cardiac arrest patient. Confirmed by waveform ETCO2 and auscultation.

resource.name: Example EMS AllergyIntolerance — Penicillin
resource.description: Example allergy demonstrating the EMSAllergyIntolerance profile. Documented penicillin allergy (eHistory.06 medication allergy) reported during MVA response.

resource.name: Example EMS Blood Pressure Observation
resource.description: Example blood pressure observation demonstrating the EMSObservationBP profile. First vital set taken at the scene of an MVA.

resource.name: Example EMS Cardiac Arrest Condition
resource.description: Example cardiac arrest Condition demonstrating the EMSConditionArrest profile. Patient found in cardiac arrest prior to EMS arrival; VF arrest; CPR and defibrillation performed; ROSC achieved prior to ED arrival.

resource.name: Example EMS Claim — ALS Level 1 Emergency
resource.description: Example ambulance claim demonstrating the EMSClaim profile. ALS Level 1 Emergency service billed to Medicare Part B for cardiac arrest transport.

resource.name: Example EMS Composition — PCR Document
resource.description: Example PCR composition demonstrating the EMSComposition profile. Assembles all resources from the MVA cardiac arrest scenario into a complete EMS Patient Care Report document.

resource.name: Example EMS Condition — Closed Head Injury
resource.description: Example condition demonstrating the EMSCondition profile. Primary impression of closed head injury documented during an MVA response.

resource.name: Example EMS Coverage — Medicare Part B
resource.description: Example insurance coverage demonstrating the EMSCoverage profile. Represents a patient with Medicare Part B as primary insurance.

resource.name: Example EMS Destination — Level I Trauma Center
resource.description: Example destination location demonstrating the EMSLocationDestination profile. Riverside University Health System Medical Center, a Level I Trauma Center.

resource.name: Example EMS Device — ALS Ambulance
resource.description: Example vehicle device demonstrating the EMSDeviceVehicle profile. Riverside County EMS Unit 4321, a Type I ALS-equipped ground ambulance.

resource.name: Example EMS Document Reference — 12-Lead ECG
resource.description: Example external document attachment demonstrating the EMSDocumentReference profile: a 12-lead ECG PDF captured during the MVA response, typed with NEMSIS 4509009 (ECG/Lab Results) and LOINC 11524-6.

resource.name: Example EMS ECG Rhythm Observation
resource.description: Cardiac rhythm observation (eVitals.03) demonstrating the ext-ems-ecg-context extension: sinus tachycardia on a 12-lead ECG with computer interpretation.

resource.name: Example EMS Encounter — MVA 911 Response
resource.description: Example EMS encounter demonstrating the EMSEncounter profile. 911 response to a motor vehicle accident with ALS transport to a Level I trauma center.

resource.description: Neurological assessment observation (LOINC 10197-4). Altered mental status findings on MVA patient.

resource.description: Skin assessment observation (LOINC 8709-8). Pale, diaphoretic findings on MVA patient.

resource.name: Example EMS GCS Panel Observation
resource.description: Example GCS total panel observation demonstrating the EMSObservationGCS profile. GCS 13 (E4V4M5) on MVA patient.

resource.name: Example EMS Home Medication — Lisinopril
resource.description: Example home-medication statement demonstrating the EMSMedicationStatement profile. Patient reports taking lisinopril 10 mg orally once daily for hypertension.

resource.name: Example EMS Injury Condition
resource.description: Example injury Condition demonstrating the EMSConditionInjury profile. 35-year-old male driver in a motor vehicle accident: blunt mechanism, restrained with front airbag deployment, significant vehicle intrusion requiring extrication, motor GCS 4, with ACN telematics data from the vehicle.

resource.name: Example EMS Lab — Blood Glucose
resource.description: Example lab observation demonstrating the EMSObservationLabs profile. Point-of-care blood glucose obtained at the scene during MVA response.

resource.name: Example EMS Medication Administration — Normal Saline Bolus
resource.description: Example medication administration demonstrating the EMSMedicationAdministration profile. Normal saline 500 mL IV bolus administered to an MVA patient.

resource.name: Example EMS Organization — Riverside County EMS
resource.description: Example EMS agency organization demonstrating the EMSOrganization profile. Represents a county EMS agency with NPI, state agency ID, and service configuration.

resource.description: Example outcome observation demonstrating the EMSObservationOutcome profile. Patient admitted as inpatient from ED following MVA with head injury.

resource.name: Example EMS Pain Score Observation
resource.description: Pain severity score (eVitals.26, LOINC 72514-3) demonstrating the ext-ems-pain-scale-type extension: 7/10 on the numeric scale.

resource.name: Example EMS Past Medical History — Hypertension
resource.description: Example past-medical-history Condition demonstrating the EMSConditionProblems profile. Patient reports essential hypertension; history obtained from the patient; tetanus immunization 2020; physical exam suggests alcohol use; last oral intake at 12:30.

resource.name: Example EMS Patient — MVA Victim
resource.description: Example patient demonstrating the EMSPatient profile. 45-year-old male involved in a motor vehicle accident.

resource.name: Example EMS Practitioner — Paramedic
resource.description: Example EMS crew member demonstrating the EMSPractitioner profile. Represents a licensed Paramedic with state certification.

resource.name: Example EMS PractitionerRole — Paramedic Crew Member
resource.description: Example PractitionerRole demonstrating the EMSPractitionerRole profile. Paramedic Maria Ramirez serving as Primary Patient Caregiver on MVA response.

resource.name: Example EMS Procedure — IV Access
resource.description: Example procedure demonstrating the EMSProcedure profile. Peripheral IV access established on an MVA patient.

resource.name: Example EMS Provenance — Transfer of Care Signature
resource.description: Example signature Provenance demonstrating the EMSProvenance profile: the EMS primary care provider signs the PCR for transfer of patient care at the receiving ED.

resource.name: Example EMS Questionnaire Response — Agency Custom Elements
resource.description: Example custom-element result demonstrating the EMSQuestionnaireResponse profile: the ambulance was decontaminated after the MVA transport (eCustomResults).

resource.name: Example EMS Questionnaire — Agency Custom Elements
resource.description: Example custom-element definition demonstrating the EMSQuestionnaire profile: an agency-defined element asking whether the ambulance was decontaminated after transport (eCustomConfiguration).

resource.description: Example scene location demonstrating the EMSLocationScene profile. Motor vehicle accident on Interstate 15 northbound near Murrieta, California.

resource.description: Stroke scale result (eVitals.29) demonstrating the ext-ems-stroke-scale-context extension and the NDR-002 pertinent negative pattern: CPSS negative (stroke findings not present).

resource.name: Example EMS Vital Sign — Heart Rate
resource.description: Heart rate observation (LOINC 8867-4). 110 bpm at scene — tachycardic MVA patient.

resource.description: Respiratory rate observation (LOINC 9279-1). 22 breaths/min — slightly tachypneic.

resource.name: Example EMS Vital Sign — SpO2
resource.description: Pulse oximetry observation (LOINC 59408-5). 94% on room air at scene.

resource.name: Example GCS Eye Opening Sub-observation
resource.description: GCS Eye Opening sub-observation (LOINC 9267-6). Score = 4 (Spontaneous).

resource.name: Example GCS Motor Response Sub-observation
resource.description: GCS Motor Response sub-observation (LOINC 9268-4). Score = 5 (Localizes pain).

resource.name: Example GCS Verbal Response Sub-observation
resource.description: GCS Verbal Response sub-observation (LOINC 9270-0). Score = 4 (Confused).

resource.name: Narrative Conformance Statements
resource.description: Conformance statements found throughout the narrative of the IG consolidated into this computable resource for traceability purposes

resource.name: NEMSIS DEM Demographic Code System
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS 3.5.1 DEM dataset sections dContact (agency contact type, medical director degree/board certification/compensation), dConfiguration (specialty service capability, EMD provision, patient monitoring capability), and dLocation (EMS location type).

resource.name: NEMSIS eAirway Code System
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS 3.5.1 eAirway section covering airway management indications, device types, confirmation methods, confirming personnel, complications, and reasons for failed management.

resource.name: NEMSIS eArrest Code System
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS 3.5.1 eArrest section covering cardiac arrest status, etiology, resuscitation attempts, CPR type, ROSC, neurological outcome, end-of-event disposition, and first-responder roles.

resource.name: NEMSIS eHistory Code System
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS 3.5.1 eHistory section covering advance directives, medical history source, immunization type, current medication dosage units and frequency, alcohol/drug use indicators, and pregnancy status.

resource.name: NEMSIS eInjury Code System
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS 3.5.1 eInjury section covering mechanism of injury, CDC trauma triage criteria (high and moderate risk), vehicle occupant location, occupant safety equipment, airbag deployment, OSHA personal protective equipment, and ACN vehicle seat location.

resource.name: NEMSIS eLabs Code System
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS 3.5.1 eLabs section covering lab result types and imaging study types.

resource.name: NEMSIS Encounter Codes
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS eResponse, eDispatch, and eDisposition sections (NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2).

resource.name: NEMSIS eOther Code System
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS 3.5.1 eOther section covering registry candidacy, crew personal protective equipment, work-related injury/exposure types, disaster types, external document types, and signature metadata (signer type, reason, patient representative, status).

resource.name: NEMSIS ePatient Codes
resource.description:
Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2 ePatient section.
Includes codes for Sex (ePatient.25), Age Units (ePatient.16),
and Alternate Home Residence (ePatient.22).
Race codes (ePatient.14) are mapped to OMB/CDC categories used in us-core-race.

resource.name: NEMSIS ePayment Code System
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS 3.5.1 ePayment section covering primary method of payment, insurance billing priority, relationship to insured, response urgency, patient transport assessment, ambulance transport reason codes, ambulance conditions indicators, CMS service level, and payer type.

resource.name: NEMSIS ePayment Payer Type Code System
resource.description: X12 payer type codes used in NEMSIS ePayment.57 to identify the type of insurance payer.

resource.name: NEMSIS General Codes
resource.description: General NEMSIS enumeration codes for sections without a dedicated CodeSystem. Currently covers eHistory (eHistory.01 - Barriers to Patient Care). NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2.

resource.name: NEMSIS Medications and Procedures Codes
resource.description: Enumeration codes for the NEMSIS eMedications and eProcedures sections. Covers administration routes, dosage units, complications, vascular access locations, airway techniques, clinical response, authorization types, caregiver levels, and Yes/No values shared across both sections. NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2.

resource.name: NEMSIS Not Value
resource.description:
Represents the NEMSIS 'Not Value' (NV) pattern used when a data element
is Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting. Maps to FHIR dataAbsentReason
patterns but preserves NEMSIS-specific codes.

resource.name: NEMSIS Not Value (NV)
resource.description: NEMSIS Not Value codes used when a data element is Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting.

resource.name: NEMSIS Not Value Codes
resource.description: NEMSIS Not Value (NV) codes used when a data element cannot be recorded. Three codes apply: NotApplicable (element does not apply to this event), NotRecorded (element applies but was not documented), NotReporting (agency is not reporting this element).

resource.name: NEMSIS Personnel Level Codes
resource.description: NEMSIS eCrew.02 - Crew Member Level certification and licensure codes (NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2).

resource.name: NEMSIS Pertinent Negative
resource.description:
Represents the NEMSIS 'Pertinent Negative' (PN) pattern for clinical data elements.
Indicates a clinically significant absence (e.g., 'Denies Chest Pain').

resource.name: NEMSIS Pertinent Negative (PN)
resource.description: NEMSIS Pertinent Negative codes indicating a clinically significant documented absence.

resource.name: NEMSIS Pertinent Negative Codes
resource.description: NEMSIS Pertinent Negative (PN) codes indicating a clinically significant documented absence. Applied to clinical data elements where documenting a negative finding is as meaningful as documenting a positive one.

resource.name: NEMSIS Physical Exam Codes
resource.description: Enumeration codes for the NEMSIS eExam physical examination section. Covers body region assessments and finding location qualifiers. NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2.

resource.name: NEMSIS Procedures/Protocols Codes
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS eProtocols section (eProtocols.01 - Protocols Used). NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2.

resource.name: NEMSIS Situation Codes
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS eSituation section (NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2).

resource.name: NEMSIS Vital Signs Codes
resource.description: Enumeration codes from the NEMSIS eVitals section (NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2).

resource.name: Patient Home Census Tract
resource.description: NEMSIS ePatient.11 - The US Census Bureau census tract for the patient's home address.

resource.name: US National Grid Coordinates
resource.description: NEMSIS dLocation.05 - The US National Grid (USNG) coordinates of an EMS location.
